adjudication_notes: Third pedestrian (dark-green uniform) is bare-headed with no hard hat on close inspection; the other two clearly wear yellow hard hats.
Workers stand on a graded, walkable earthen embankment, not a sheer vertical edge -- a normal site slope, not the kind of edge the guardrail rule targets.
Workers walk the flat road set back from the crest of a steep, rocky graded slope; nobody is positioned at an exposed vertical edge, though the loose/displaced panel below leaves some residual doubt.
Worker in hi-vis stands to the side/front of the excavator while it digs, outside the apparent swing arc; exact distance and machine swing state can't be fully confirmed from a static frame.

Two workers are actively climbing an unprotected, steep reinforced slope face by hand -- a real fall-while-climbing exposure, distinct from standing on a walkable graded batter elsewhere in this batch.
Workers are down at the base of the embankment, not positioned at the crest; no one is visibly exposed to an unprotected edge in this frame.
Worker stands on flat ground next to graded embankments; no vertical drop-off is present at his position.
Excavation walls are visibly sloped/graded, and the only clearly visible workers are on flat ground at top, away from any crest.
Faint figures are visible only far in the background near a parked truck, clearly separated from the excavators actively digging in the foreground; no worker is close to active equipment.

No worker on the crew has a visible harness, but the frame doesn't establish real height/fall exposure (no edge, drop, or multi-story context visible) -- can't confirm this is fall-risk work rather than a low-elevation slab pour.

On close crop, a clearly-present individual (redacted/blurred per this dataset's face-blur convention) stands immediately beside the excavator's arm/cylinder assembly while the bucket is actively digging -- unsafely close to moving equipment.
Extreme close-up crop on the bucket and rock pile shows no worker at all and no surrounding context; can't judge whether anyone is nearby just outside the frame.
